justification: All rubric criteria met: Event sourcing correctly identified as appropriate but not the only option (dual-write audit log, CDC, temporal tables all discussed). Schema evolution risk clearly identified with upcasting as mitigation. Snapshotting requirement explicitly addressed with dedicated Snapshot Service component. Simpler audit-log alternative presented in trade_offs alternatives_considered. trade_offs includes substantial gains and costs. implementation_guidance distinguishes approaches and provides phased rollout.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough trade_offs section with three distinct decisions analyzed, each with multiple alternatives considered including the simpler audit-log-table approach
Schema evolution addressed in multiple places: rigidity_points, flexibility_points (upcasting), and pitfalls_to_avoid
Snapshotting covered as both a dedicated component and a risk mitigation for unbounded event store growth
GDPR/crypto-shredding risk identified proactively — goes beyond the rubric requirements
Domain model with event storming, bounded contexts, and concrete event types demonstrates deep understanding
executive_summary leans toward recommending event sourcing rather than presenting both options neutrally, though alternatives are thoroughly covered in the body
